item.title: 【Code Security Remediation: What 50,000 Repositories Reveal About PR Scanning】代码安全修复：50,000 个存储库揭示了 PR 扫描的内容
item.description:
【

Security teams have gotten good at finding vulnerabilities. Fixing them has always been the hard part. An analysis of remediation patterns across 50,000+ actively developed repositories and 400+ organizations during 2025 reveals a pattern: where a vulnerability is detected has more impact on whether it gets fixed than what the vulnerability is.

PR-Detected Findings Get Fixed 9x Faster


】

安全团队非常善于发现漏洞。修复它们一直是困难的部分。对 2025 年 50,000 多个积极开发的存储库和 400 多个组织的修复模式的分析揭示了一种模式：检测到漏洞的位置对漏洞是否得到修复的影响比漏洞本身的影响更大。

公关检测结果的修复速度提高了 9 倍

item.title: 【When Kubernetes Breaks Session Consistency: Using Cosmos DB and Redis Together】当 Kubernetes 破坏会话一致性时：同时使用 Cosmos DB 和 Redis
item.description:
【

Distributed systems rarely struggle because of storage engines. They struggle because of coordination.

We were operating a high-throughput microservice on Kubernetes backed by Azure Cosmos DB. The service required durability, global availability, and predictable read behavior under horizontal scaling. Cosmos DB was configured with SESSION consistency because it offers a practical balance between correctness and performance. It guarantees read-your-own-writes without incurring the latency and throughput penalties associated with strong consistency.

】

分布式系统很少会因为存储引擎而陷入困境。他们因为协调而挣扎。

我们正在运行由 Azure Cosmos DB 支持的高吞吐量Kubernetes 上的微服务。该服务需要持久性、全局可用性以及水平扩展下的可预测读取行为。 Cosmos DB 配置了 SESSION 一致性，因为它提供了正确性和性能之间的实际平衡。它保证读取您自己的写入，而不会产生与强一致性相关的延迟和吞吐量损失。

item.title: 【How to Reliably Implement Post-Commit Actions in Spring】如何在 Spring 中可靠地实现提交后操作
item.description:
【

Sometimes, in modern backend systems, you need to perform one or more actions after database inserts or updates. You may need to publish to a message broker, send an email, or trigger a workflow. If you perform those actions inside a database transaction that rolls back at the end, it is too late, because they are already started and cannot be cancelled, possibly creating inconsistencies. In Spring Boot, you can use @TransactionalEventListener to mark a listener that is triggered by the ApplicationEventPublisher's publish method to solve this problem. In this article, we will explain how this works.

The Problem

In this article, we are discussing the side effects associated with transactional code.  By side effects, we mean deliberate actions towards external services and resources. As an example, we can consider an application component that creates an order, saves it in the database, and then sends an email:

】

有时，在现代后端系统中，您需要在数据库插入或更新后执行一项或多项操作。您可能需要发布到消息代理、发送电子邮件或触发工作流程。如果您在最后回滚的数据库事务中执行这些操作，那就太晚了，因为它们已经启动并且无法取消，可能会造成不一致。在 Spring Boot 中，您可以使用 @TransactionalEventListener 标记由 ApplicationEventPublisher的publish方法可以解决这个问题。在本文中，我们将解释其工作原理。

问题

在本文中，我们将讨论与事务代码相关的副作用。  所谓副作用，是指对外部服务和资源采取刻意的行动。作为示例，我们可以考虑一个应用程序组件，它创建订单，将其保存在数据库中，然后发送电子邮件：

item.title: 【Architecting the Future of Research: A Technical Deep-Dive into NotebookLM and Gemini Integration】构建研究的未来：对 NotebookLM 和 Gemini 集成的技术深入探讨
item.description:
【

In the rapidly evolving landscape of large language models (LLMs), the challenge has shifted from generating text to managing context. As developers and researchers, we are often overwhelmed not by a lack of information, but by the inability to synthesize vast amounts of heterogeneous data efficiently. Enter NotebookLM, a specialized research environment, and the underlying Gemini 1.5 Pro architecture. Together, they represent a paradigm shift in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and personal knowledge management.

This article explores the technical foundations of NotebookLM, the mechanics of its integration with Gemini 1.5 Pro, and how to build production-grade content pipelines using these tools.

】

在大型语言模型 (LLM) 快速发展的格局中，挑战已从生成文本转变为管理上下文。作为开发人员和研究人员，我们常常不是因为缺乏信息而不知所措，而是因为无法有效地合成大量异构数据。输入 NotebookLM，这是一个专门的研究环境，以及底层的 Gemini 1.5 Pro 架构。它们共同代表了检索增强生成 (RAG) 和个人知识管理的范式转变。

本文探讨了 NotebookLM 的技术基础、其与 Gemini 1.5 Pro 集成的机制，以及如何使用这些工具构建生产级内容管道。

item.title: 【The Platform or the Pile: How GitOps and Developer Platforms Are Settling the Infrastructure Debt Reckoning】平台还是一堆：GitOps 和开发者平台如何解决基础设施债务清算
item.description:
【

There is a specific kind of organizational dysfunction that doesn't show up in sprint velocity metrics or deployment frequency dashboards. It lives in Slack threads where a senior engineer is, for the third time this week, helping a product team figure out why their staging environment behaves differently from production. It lives in the postmortem where someone admits, with genuine embarrassment, that a misconfigured resource limit brought down a service because the relevant YAML file was copied from a two-year-old deployment that nobody remembers creating. It lives in the quiet calculation a platform team lead makes when she realizes her team of six is fielding forty tickets a week, almost none of which required human judgment, and almost all of which could have been prevented by infrastructure that didn't exist yet.

This dysfunction has a name now, though it took the industry a while to agree on one. Platform engineering. The practice of building deliberate, opinionated abstractions between developers and the underlying complexity of modern infrastructure. And in 2025, it stopped being a trend and started being a reckoning.

】

有一种特定类型的组织功能障碍不会出现在冲刺速度指标或部署频率仪表板中。它存在于 Slack 线程中，高级工程师本周第三次帮助产品团队弄清楚为什么他们的登台环境的行为与生产环境不同。它存在于事后剖析中，有人非常尴尬地承认，错误配置的资源限制导致了服务瘫痪，因为相关的 YAML 文件是从一个两年前的部署复制而来的，而没有人记得创建过该文件。当平台团队负责人意识到她的六人团队每周处理四十张罚单时，她会进行安静的计算，其中几乎不需要人类判断，而且几乎所有这些都可以通过尚不存在的基础设施来阻止。

这种功能障碍现在有了一个名字，尽管业界花了一段时间才达成一致。 平台工程。在开发人员和现代基础设施的潜在复杂性之间构建深思熟虑的、固执己见的抽象的实践。到了 2025 年，它不再是一种趋势，而是开始成为一种清算。

item.title: 【Microsoft Fabric AI Functions: A Practical Overview for Data Engineers】Microsoft Fabric AI 函数：数据工程师实用概述
item.description:
【

In this article, we will focus on AI functions in Microsoft Fabric Spark Notebooks. Then, we will observe in depth the most interesting of them. Our focus will be on their practical use in real-world scenarios and on assessing production readiness. So, we will also validate their accuracy and performance. The code will be added to support those who want to check how these AI functions work in their own environment, with minimal development time.

AI Functions in Microsoft Fabric: My Overview

What are the AI functions in Microsoft Fabric Notebooks? Their purpose is to make it easy to introduce the large language model's data-processing capabilities into Spark Notebooks data processing in Fabric. As a result, with minimal code and configuration, a wide range of people, from data analysts to data engineers, can use them and achieve the necessary practical results.

】

在本文中，我们将重点介绍 Microsoft Fabric Spark 笔记本中的 AI 功能。然后，我们将深入观察其中最有趣的部分。我们的重点将放在它们在现实场景中的实际使用以及评估生产准备情况。因此，我们还将验证它们的准确性和性能。添加代码是为了支持那些想要以最短的开发时间检查这些人工智能功能在自己的环境中如何工作的人。

Microsoft Fabric 中的 AI 功能：我的概述

Microsoft Fabric Notebooks 中有哪些 AI 功能？它们的目的是轻松地将大型语言模型的数据处理功能引入 Fabric 中的 Spark Notebooks 数据处理中。因此，通过最少的代码和配置，从数据分析师到数据工程师的广泛人员都可以使用它们并获得必要的实际结果。

item.title: 【Runtime FinOps: Making Cloud Cost Observable】运行时 FinOps：使云成本可观察
item.description:
【

There's a particular kind of learned helplessness that settles into engineering organizations after a few years of rapid cloud growth. You ship a feature. The feature works. Latency looks fine, error rates stay quiet, on-call doesn't page. Then three weeks later someone from finance drops a Slack message — a screenshot of the AWS Cost Explorer with a jagged upward spike, annotated with a red arrow and a question mark. By then, the deployment that caused it has been buried under six more deploys. The engineer who wrote the change is mentally two features ahead. Nobody remembers. You run a postmortem on nothing.

This is the default state for most shops. Not negligence, exactly. More like a structural information deficit: the feedback loop between code change and cost impact is measured in billing cycles, not seconds.

】

经过几年的云的快速增长之后，工程组织中出现了一种特殊的习得性无助感。您发布了一个功能。该功能有效。延迟看起来不错，错误率保持较低，待命状态也不会寻呼。三周后，财务部门的某人发出了一条 Slack 消息——一张 AWS Cost Explorer 的屏幕截图，上面有锯齿状的向上尖峰，并带有红色箭头和问号注释。到那时，导致它的部署已被另外六次部署所掩盖。编写更改的工程师在心理上领先于两个功能。没有人记得。你什么都不做事后剖析。

这是大多数商店的默认状态。确切地说，不是疏忽。更像是结构性信息赤字：代码更改和成本影响之间的反馈循环是以计费周期而不是秒来衡量的。

item.title: 【Mastering Gemma 4】掌握杰玛 4
item.description:
【

Large language models (LLMs) have shifted dramatically from monolithic, proprietary APIs toward highly efficient, open-weight models that developers can run on commodity hardware. Google’s Gemma series has been at the forefront of this movement. With the release of Gemma 4, the industry sees a significant leap in performance-per-parameter, driven by advanced distillation techniques and architectural refinements that challenge models twice its size.

In this deep dive, we will explore the technical underpinnings of Gemma 4, its unique training methodology, and practical strategies for integrating it into your production environment.

】

大型语言模型 (LLM) 已从单一的专有 API 急剧转变为开发人员可以在商用硬件上运行的高效、开放权重模型。谷歌的 Gemma 系列一直走在这一运动的最前沿。随着 Gemma 4 的发布，在先进的蒸馏技术和架构改进的推动下，业界看到了每参数性能的显着飞跃，挑战了两倍大小的模型。

在本次深入研究中，我们将探索 Gemma 4 的技术基础、其独特的培训方法以及将其集成到生产环境中的实用策略。

item.title: 【AI-Driven DevOps for SaaS: From Reactive to Predictive Pipelines】AI 驱动的 SaaS DevOps：从反应式管道到预测式管道
item.description:
【

Modern SaaS companies live and die by their ability to deliver new features quickly without breaking the service for users. DevOps practices brought automation and velocity to software delivery but they traditionally operate in a reactive way responding to failures or performance issues after they occur. Today, Artificial Intelligence is reshaping this paradigm. By infusing machine learning and automation into CI/CD and operations DevOps is evolving from simple scripted workflows into intelligent, self-optimizing pipelines that can predict and prevent problems before they impact customers. Analysts even predict that by 2027 over 50% of enterprise teams will have AI agents embedded in their pipelines to boost speed, quality, and governance. Early adopters are already seeing 20–30% faster delivery and 40% fewer defects in releases by augmenting development with AI-driven tools. In a SaaS context where continuous updates and 24/7 uptime are critical moving from reactive to predictive pipelines is becoming a game changer.

From Reactive Automation to Predictive DevOps

Traditional DevOps automation follows a reactive model run predefined scripts, deploy on schedule, and fire alerts when something goes wrong. This approach is fast, but not intelligent pipelines don’t learn from past failures or adapt to new conditions. AI-driven DevOps flips this script by adding prediction, learning, and adaptation on top of automation. Instead of merely doing what it’s told, an AI-augmented system can analyze data from builds, tests, and production telemetry to anticipate what might go wrong and act accordingly.

】

现代 SaaS 公司的生死存亡取决于其在不中断用户服务的情况下快速提供新功能的能力。 DevOps 实践为软件交付带来了自动化和速度，但它们传统上以被动方式运行，以响应故障或性能问题发生后的情况。如今，人工智能正在重塑这一范式。通过将机器学习和自动化融入到 CI/CD 和运营中，DevOps 正在从简单的脚本化工作流程发展成为智能、自我优化的管道，可以在问题影响客户之前预测和预防问题。分析师甚至预测，到 2027 年，超过 50% 的企业团队将在其管道中嵌入人工智能代理，以提高速度、质量和治理。通过使用人工智能驱动的工具增强开发，早期采用者已经看到交付速度提高了 20-30%，发布缺陷减少了 40%。在持续更新和 24/7 正常运行时间至关重要的 SaaS 环境中，从反应性管道转向预测性管道正在成为游戏规则的改变者。

从反应式自动化到预测性 DevOps

传统 DevOps 自动化遵循反应式模型，运行预定义脚本、按计划部署，并在出现问题时发出警报。这种方法速度很快，但不智能管道无法从过去的失败中学习或适应新的条件。 人工智能驱动的 DevOps 通过在自动化之上添加预测、学习和适应来翻转此脚本。人工智能增强系统不仅可以按照指示行事，还可以分析来自构建、测试和生产遥测的数据，以预测可能出现什么问题并采取相应行动。

item.title: 【NeMo Agent Toolkit With Docker Model Runner】NeMo Agent 工具包与 Docker 模型运行器
item.description:
【

The year 2025 has been widely recognized as the year of AI agents. With the launch of frameworks like Docker Cagent, Microsoft Agent Framework (MAF), and Google’s Agent Development Kit (ADK), organizations rapidly embraced agentic systems.

However, one critical area received far less attention: agent observability.

】

2025 年被广泛认为是人工智能代理年。随着 Docker Cagent、Microsoft Agent Framework (MAF) 和 Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) 等框架的推出，组织迅速采用了代理系统。

但是，有一个关键领域受到的关注要少得多：代理可观察性。
